description: Push Chain is a universal L1 blockchain enabling apps to deploy once and execute across all supported chains. Users sign from any wallet; the SDK handles cross-chain routing, UEA resolution, and fee abstraction automatically.

files.purpose: Machine-readable index of all files in /agents/, entry point for discovery
Agent-oriented orientation guide explaining how to navigate /agents/ for different tasks
Structured list of what Push Chain SDK can do, mapped to relevant workflows and examples
SDK method signatures and parameters for @pushchain/core and @pushchain/ui-kit
List of supported chains with chain IDs, CAIP-10 identifiers, and network status
Feature availability matrix across chains, networks, and SDK versions
All PushChain.CONSTANTS values with descriptions and usage context
Structured decision tree for selecting the correct workflow based on task requirements
Human-readable guide for routing user intents to the correct workflow or example
Catalog of SDK error codes, meanings, and recommended recovery actions
Step-by-step recovery procedures for common failure scenarios
MCP tool definitions for Push Chain operations, ready for Model Context Protocol integration
Mapping of documentation sources to their last-verified dates and authority levels
Version history of /agents/ layer changes and SDK updates
Semantic mapping of concepts to file paths for RAG and retrieval systems
Index of all workflow files with summaries and prerequisite chains
Workflow for initializing PushChainClient from any supported wallet type
Primary workflow for sending transactions to Push Chain (Route 1)
Workflow for sending transactions to external chains via CEA (Route 2 and Route 3)
Workflow for tracking transaction status and handling confirmations
Workflow for integrating wallet connection UI using @pushchain/ui-kit
Workflow for signing messages with a UniversalSigner
Index of all JSON schema files with brief descriptions
JSON schema for UniversalAccount object (address + chain in CAIP-10 format)
JSON schema for UniversalSigner object with signing method signatures
JSON schema for transaction request object passed to sendTransaction
JSON schema for transaction response returned from sendTransaction
JSON schema for transaction receipt after confirmation
JSON schema for chain configuration objects
JSON schema for SDK error objects
Full index of all 60+ code examples with tags, prerequisites, and difficulty levels
Example: Initialize PushChainClient using ethers.js signer
Example: Initialize PushChainClient using Solana Keypair
Example: Send a value transfer to Push Chain (Route 1)
Example: Send a transaction to an external chain via CEA (Route 2)
Example: Track transaction status with progress hooks
Example: Set up PushUniversalWalletProvider in a React app
Example: Sign a message using UniversalSigner

canonical_workflows.name: Initialize and Send First Transaction
canonical_workflows.description: Create UniversalSigner, initialize PushChainClient, send a transaction to Push Chain. Start here for most integrations.

canonical_workflows.name: Send Transaction to External Chain (Route 2)
canonical_workflows.description: Execute on Ethereum, Solana, or other supported chains via Chain Executor Account. User signs once; SDK handles routing.

canonical_workflows.name: Initialize Client from Any Wallet
canonical_workflows.description: Convert ethers, viem, or Solana signers to UniversalSigner and initialize the SDK client.

authority_ranking.notes: Primary source of truth for SDK usage, method signatures, and transaction flows
Complete text of all documentation pages, use for deep context or when specific page content is needed
Installation instructions and quick examples, may lag behind docs
Conceptual explanations and announcements, not API reference
